skill.name: youtube-transcript-extractor-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube video transcripts, titles, likes, and channel metadata via BrowserAct API. Use when users need video subtitles for analysis, summarization, or dataset building.
skill.summary: Extract YouTube video transcripts and metadata via BrowserAct API
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill uses environment variable access for API key configuration and makes outbound HTTP requests to the BrowserAct API. These are legitimate patterns for external API integrations. No command injection, code execution, or credential exfiltration risks were confirmed. The skill is safe for publication with low risk profile.

medium_findings.title: API Key Required for Operation
medium_findings.description: The skill requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable to function. This is legitimate credential management, not a security risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: API key access via os.getenv is standard pattern for credential management. Key is used only for authenticated BrowserAct API calls.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Endpoint URL
low_findings.description: The script uses a hardcoded BrowserAct API base URL. This is expected for a skill designed to work with a specific external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded API endpoint is appropriate for a skill designed to integrate with a specific service. URL is a known legitimate service.

content.value_statement: Users struggle to extract text content from YouTube videos efficiently for analysis. This skill connects to the BrowserAct API to automatically fetch transcripts, titles, likes counts, and channel metadata from any YouTube video URL.
actual_capabilities: Extract complete transcript from any YouTube video URL
Retrieve video metadata including title, likes count, and publisher information
Get channel details and channel link from video URL
Return structured data ready for AI processing and summarization
Handle errors gracefully with clear user feedback
Provide status updates during long-running tasks
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key (BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable)
Cannot extract transcripts from private or age-restricted videos
Task may take several minutes for automated browser operations

use_cases.title: Video Content Analysis
use_cases.description: Extract transcript text from YouTube videos for AI-powered analysis, summarization, or sentiment analysis without manual transcription.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers, content analysts, and data scientists

use_cases.title: Dataset Building
use_cases.description: Automate collection of video transcripts and metadata to build training datasets for machine learning models.

use_cases.title: Content Summarization Pipeline
use_cases.description: Integrate transcript extraction into automated workflows that feed video content into summarization or note-taking systems.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Transcript Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the youtube-transcript-extractor-api-skill to extract the transcript from this YouTube video: [VIDEO_URL]
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a YouTube URL and wants the full transcript

prompt_templates.title: Extract with Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the transcript, title, likes count, and channel information from [VIDEO_URL] using the youtube-transcript-extractor-api-skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants full video details including engagement metrics

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to extract transcripts from multiple YouTube videos for analysis. Set up a workflow using youtube-transcript-extractor-api-skill to process these URLs: [URL_1], [URL_2], [URL_3]
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to process multiple videos in sequence

prompt_templates.title: Summarization Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use youtube-transcript-extractor-api-skill to get the transcript from [VIDEO_URL], then summarize the key points.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants both extraction and AI summarization

output_examples.output: Returns video title, publisher, channel link, likes count, and complete transcript text ready for analysis

output_examples.input: Get metadata for this video without watching it
output_examples.output: Provides structured data with video_title, publisher, video_likes_count, and transcript in clean text format

output_examples.input: Build a dataset from these video transcripts
output_examples.output: Sequential extraction produces clean transcript text from multiple videos, formatted for dataset use

best_practices: Always verify BROWSERACT_API_KEY is configured before attempting extraction
Check error messages for authorization issues versus task failures to retry appropriately
Allow sufficient time for task completion as automated browser operations may take several minutes
anti_patterns: Do not attempt extraction without a valid API key configured
Do not retry on authorization errors; guide user to check API key instead
Do not assume transcripts exist for all videos (private or restricted videos may fail)

faq.question: What API key do I need?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API key. Sign up at browseract.com and get your key from the integrations page.

faq.question: Does this work with private videos?
faq.answer: No. This skill cannot extract transcripts from private, unlisted, or age-restricted YouTube videos.

faq.question: How long does extraction take?
faq.answer: The automated browser operations typically take 1-5 minutes depending on video length and server load.

faq.question: What data does it return?
faq.answer: It returns the video title, publisher name, channel link, likes count, and the complete transcript text.

faq.question: Can I extract from multiple videos?
faq.answer: Yes. Call the skill multiple times with different URLs, or use the skill in a loop for batch processing.

faq.question: What happens if the task fails?
faq.answer: If it is an authorization error, do not retry. For other errors, the skill will retry once automatically before reporting the issue.
